---
applyTo: "cmd/**/*.go"
---

# Goca Command & Code Generation Rules

## Command Structure Pattern

Every `goca <command>` follows this exact initialization sequence. Never deviate:

```go
var myCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "mycommand <name>",
    Short: "One-line description",
    Long:  `Multi-line description.`,
    Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
    Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
        name := args[0]

        // 1. Parse flags
        flagValue, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("flag-name")

        // 2. Load config
        configIntegration := NewConfigIntegration()
        if err := configIntegration.LoadConfigForProject(); err != nil {
            fmt.Printf("Warning: Could not load configuration: %v\n", err)
        }

        // 3. Merge CLI flags with config (CLI takes precedence)
        flags := map[string]interface{}{"flag-name": flagValue}
        configIntegration.MergeWithCLIFlags(flags)

        // 4. Get effective values
        effectiveValue := configIntegration.GetEffectiveValue(flagValue)

        // 5. Initialize safety manager
        dryRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
        force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
        backup, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("backup")
        safetyMgr := NewSafetyManager(dryRun, force, backup)

        // 6. Validate inputs
        validator := NewCommandValidator()
        if err := validator.ValidateEntityName(name); err != nil {
            validator.errorHandler.HandleError(err, "name validation")
        }

        // 7. Generate
        // ...

        // 8. Report results
        safetyMgr.PrintSummary()
    },
}
```

## Template Writing Rules

Templates are Go `text/template` strings. Follow these rules strictly:

### Template Data Access

Always use `TemplateData` struct fields — never raw strings:

```go
// CORRECT
type TemplateData struct {
    Entity  EntityData
    Fields  []FieldData
    Module  string    // from go.mod or .goca.yaml
    Database string
    Features FeatureFlags
}

// In templates: reference as {{.Entity.Name}}, {{.Module}}, {{.Fields}}
```

### Import Deduplication

Templates that conditionally import packages MUST use the `{{.Imports}}` field — never hardcode imports:

```go
const entityTemplate = `package {{.Entity.Package}}

import (
{{- range .Imports}}
	"{{.}}"
{{- end}}
)
// ...
`
```

### GORM Tag Generation

GORM tags must follow a consistent format:

```go
// Basic: gorm:"type:varchar(255);not null"
// Primary key: gorm:"primaryKey;autoIncrement"
// Soft delete: gorm:"softDelete"
// Unique: gorm:"uniqueIndex"
```

### Validation Tag Generation

Use `validate` struct tags with `github.com/go-playground/validator` conventions:

```go
// Required string: validate:"required,min=1,max=255"
// Required int: validate:"required,gte=0"
// Optional: validate:"omitempty,min=0"
// Email: validate:"required,email"
```

## SafetyManager Integration

Every file write MUST go through `SafetyManager`. Never call `os.WriteFile` directly in command code:

```go
// CORRECT
if err := safetyMgr.WriteFile(filePath, content); err != nil {
    fmt.Printf("Error writing %s: %v\n", filePath, err)
    os.Exit(1)
}

// FORBIDDEN
os.WriteFile(filePath, []byte(content), 0644)
```

## Path Construction

Always use `filepath.Join` — never string concatenation for paths:

```go
// CORRECT
outputPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, "internal", "domain", entityFile)

// FORBIDDEN
outputPath := projectRoot + "/internal/domain/" + entityFile
```

## Generated File Header

Every generated Go file must start with a consistent header (included in templates):

```go
// Code generated by goca. DO NOT EDIT by hand.
// Source: goca <command> <name>
// Regenerate: goca <command> <name> [flags]
package <package>
```

## Adding a New Command Checklist

When implementing any new command:

- [ ] `cobra.Command` with `Use`, `Short`, `Long`, `Args` defined
- [ ] Config integration: `NewConfigIntegration()` + `LoadConfigForProject()` + `MergeWithCLIFlags()`
- [ ] Safety manager: `NewSafetyManager(dryRun, force, backup)`
- [ ] Input validation: `NewCommandValidator()` with specific validation method
- [ ] All flag parsing done at top before any logic
- [ ] All file writes through `safetyMgr.WriteFile()`
- [ ] `--dry-run`, `--force`, `--backup` flags registered
- [ ] Command registered in `root.go` with `rootCmd.AddCommand()`
- [ ] Unit test `cmd/<command>_test.go`
- [ ] Integration test `internal/testing/tests/<command>_test.go`
- [ ] Docs page `docs/commands/<command>.md`
- [ ] `docs/commands/index.md` table updated
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` updated
- [ ] `go build ./...` passes — zero errors
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes — zero warnings

## TemplateData Population

Build `TemplateData` completely before passing to any template render function:

```go
data := TemplateData{
    Entity: EntityData{
        Name:       toPascalCase(name),
        NameLower:  toLowerCamelCase(name),
        NamePlural: pluralize(name),
        Package:    "domain",
    },
    Fields:   parsedFields,
    Module:   moduleFromConfig,
    Database: effectiveDatabase,
    Features: FeatureFlags{
        Validation:    effectiveValidation,
        BusinessRules: effectiveBusinessRules,
        Timestamps:    effectiveTimestamps,
        SoftDelete:    effectiveSoftDelete,
    },
    Imports: deduplicatedImports,
}
```

## DependencyManager Usage

Add dependencies AFTER all files are written successfully, not before:

```go
// 1. Write all files first
for _, file := range filesToWrite {
    if err := safetyMgr.WriteFile(file.Path, file.Content); err != nil {
        // handle
    }
}

// 2. Then add dependencies
if needsGorm {
    depMgr.AddDependency("gorm.io/gorm", "latest")
}
depMgr.RunGoGet()
```
